warning: AI-assisted monitoring. Verify against official legislation before product approval, export, label declaration, or customer compliance confirmation.

rules.limit: Verification required via Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA) Online Database
rules.sourceTitle: Food Additives DB (GSFA Online) | CODEXALIMENTARIUS FAO-WHO
rules.notes: The Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used in all foods. Specific provisions for food additives are available in the searchable GSFA Online Database, which is regularly updated. Users are encouraged to consult the Preamble for interpretation.
rules.evidenceQuote: The "Codex General Standard for Food Additives" (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used in all foods... This database provides, in a searchable format, all the provisions for food additives that have been adopted by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

rules.limit: Verification required via Codex General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and decisions by the Codex Committee on Contaminants in Food (CCCF)
rules.notes: Codex establishes Maximum Levels (MLs) and Guideline Levels for contaminants and naturally occurring toxicants in food and feed to protect human health. These levels are recommended by the Codex Alimentarius Commission to be legally permitted and are detailed in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and through the work of the Codex Committee on Contaminants in Food (CCCF).
rules.evidenceQuote: The Codex maximum level (ML) for a contaminant in a food or feed commodity is the maximum concentration of that substance recommended by the Codex Alimentarius Commission to be legally permitted in that commodity. ... The Codex Committee on Contaminants in Food (CCCF) establishes and endorses permitted maximum levels or guideline levels for contaminants and naturally occurring toxicants in food and feed.

rules.limit: Verification required via Codex General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995)
rules.notes: Codex has established Maximum Levels (MLs) for specific contaminants including mycotoxins, metals such as arsenic, lead, and mercury, and radionuclides. These MLs are designed to ensure food does not contain contaminants at levels which could threaten human health and are detailed in relevant Codex texts.
rules.evidenceQuote: Codex has established 17 (MLs) for contaminants including mycotoxins (poisonous funghi produced by certain chemical compounds), metals (such as arsenic, lead and mercury) and radionuclides (e.g. in drinking water)

rules.parameter: Food additives and their conditions of use
rules.limit: Verification required via database/regulation
rules.notes: Specific approved food additives and their conditions of use, including limits, must be verified by consulting the EU Additives Database, which is based on Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008.
rules.evidenceQuote: This database can serve as a tool to inform about the food additives approved for use in food in the EU and their conditions of use. It is based on the Union list of food additives. This list is in Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008.

rules.parameter: Food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, toxins, and metabolites (e.g., Salmonella, Listeria monocytogenes, Enterobacter sakazakii, staphylococcal enterotoxins, histamine)
rules.limit: Verification required via regulation
rules.sourceTitle: Microbiological criteria - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: Specific microbiological criteria for foods, including limits for various pathogens and their toxins, must be verified by consulting Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005. This regulation also includes process hygiene criteria.
rules.evidenceQuote: Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005 on microbiological criteria for foods, applicable from 1 January 2006, lays down food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, their toxins and metabolites, such as Salmonella , Listeria monocytogenes , Enterobacter sakazakii , staphylococcal enterotoxins and histamine in specific foods.

rules.parameter: General labelling requirements for prepackaged foodstuffs
rules.limit: Verification required from GSO 9:2022
rules.sourceTitle: GSO 9:2022 لائحة فنية خليجية --> بطاقات المواد الغذائية المعبأة (Labels of prepackaged foodstuffs)
rules.notes: This technical regulation covers mandatory labelling requirements, which typically include allergen declarations, nutritional information (including sodium), ingredients list, net content, and manufacturer details.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 9:2022 لائحة فنية خليجية --> بطاقات المواد الغذائية المعبأة

rules.limit: Verification required from GSO 2055-1:2015 for specific criteria

rules.notes: This technical regulation outlines the general requirements for food to be considered Halal, impacting ingredients, processing, and labelling for Halal claims.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 2055-1:2015 لائحة فنية خليجية --> الأغذية الحلال – الجزء الأول : الاشتراطات العامة للأغذية الحلال

rules.limit: Verification required
rules.notes: Compliance with MHLW regulations regarding food additives is required. Additives must be specified by MHLW as unlikely to cause harm.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food Additives

rules.parameter: Production, processing, use, cooking, or preservation methods for food/additives
rules.limit: Must conform to MHLW criteria
rules.notes: Food or additives must not be produced, processed, used, cooked, or preserved using methods that do not conform to MHLW criteria.
rules.evidenceQuote: food or additives must not be produced, processed, used, cooked, or preserved using methods that do not conform to such criteria;

rules.limit: Verification required (MHLW approval)
rules.notes: Additives (excluding natural flavoring agents and generally consumed substances used as additives), preparations, and food containing additives must not be sold, produced, imported, processed, used, stored, or displayed for sale, except when MHLW specifies them as unlikely to cause harm.
rules.evidenceQuote: Additives... and preparations and food containing additives must not be sold... except for cases that the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare specifies as unlikely to cause harm to human health...

rules.limit: Verification required (Positive List System)
rules.notes: Compliance with the 'Positive List System for Agricultural Chemical Residues in Food' is required.
rules.evidenceQuote: Positive List System for Agricultural Chemical Residues in Food

rules.parameter: Contamination or foreign substances in food/additives
rules.limit: Prohibited for sale
rules.notes: Prohibited for sale, collection, production, import, processing, use, cooking, storage, or display for sale, if likely to cause harm due to contamination or the addition of foreign substances or on other grounds.
rules.evidenceQuote: The following food and additives must not be sold... (iv) those which are likely to cause harm to human health due to contamination or the addition of foreign substances or on other grounds.

rules.limit: Must conform to MHLW standards
rules.notes: Food or additives that do not conform to MHLW criteria must not be sold or imported. Food or additives that do not conform to MHLW standards must not be produced, imported, processed, used, cooked, preserved, or sold.
rules.evidenceQuote: food or additives that do not conform to such criteria must not be sold or imported; and food or additives that do not conform to such standards must not be produced, imported, processed, used, cooked, preserved, or sold.

rules.parameter: Residues of agricultural chemicals, feed additives, pharmaceuticals for animals
rules.limit: Must not exceed MHLW-specified quantities
rules.notes: Food containing residues exceeding MHLW-specified quantities (deemed unlikely to cause harm) must not be produced, imported, processed, used, cooked, preserved for sale, or sold. This does not apply if standards concerning ingredients of food under paragraph (1) have been established for the residual limit.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food in which substances that are the ingredients of agricultural chemicals... feed... and pharmaceutical products... remain in a quantity exceeding the quantity that the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare specifies as unlikely to cause harm to human health... must not be produced, imported... or sold;

rules.parameter: Toxic or harmful substances in food/additives
rules.limit: Prohibited for sale
rules.notes: Prohibited for sale, collection, production, import, processing, use, cooking, storage, or display for sale, unless MHLW specifies they are unlikely to cause harm.
rules.evidenceQuote: The following food and additives must not be sold... (ii) those which contain or bear toxic or harmful substances or are suspected to contain or bear such substances; provided, however, that this does not apply when the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare specifies that the substances are unlikely to cause harm to human health;

rules.parameter: General business operator responsibility for safety
rules.limit: N/A
rules.notes: Food business operators must endeavor to ensure the safety of food, additives, apparatus, or containers and packaging, obtain necessary knowledge/skills, ensure raw material safety, conduct voluntary inspections, and take other necessary measures.
rules.evidenceQuote: A food business operator... must endeavor to ensure the safety of the food... and for that purpose, they must endeavor to obtain the knowledge and skills necessary... ensure the safety of raw materials... conduct voluntary inspections... and take other necessary measures.

rules.limit: N/A
rules.notes: Food business operators must endeavor to take appropriate and prompt measures, such as providing records to authorities and disposing of hazardous food, to prevent food sanitation hazards.
rules.evidenceQuote: A food business operator must endeavor to take any necessary measures appropriately and promptly, such as the provision of the record... and the disposal of the food for sale, etc. which caused the food sanitation hazards.

rules.limit: Verification required
rules.notes: Food and additives specified by MHLW Order as requiring 'certification of the conditions of food sanitation management at the production site' must not be imported for sale unless accompanied by certificates from the exporting country's governmental organization.
rules.evidenceQuote: The food and additives specified by Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare Order as requiring certification of the conditions of food sanitation management at the production site... must not be imported for the purpose of sale, unless they have certificates or copies thereof attached which have been issued by a governmental organization of an exporting country...

rules.limit: Verification required
rules.notes: Food and additives specified by MHLW Order as requiring 'measures to control particularly important processes' must not be imported for sale unless produced/processed in MHLW-specified countries/regions/facilities that actually take those measures.
rules.evidenceQuote: The food and additives specified by Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare Order as requiring measures to control particularly important processes... must not be imported for the purpose of sale, unless they have been produced or processed in the countries or regions, or facilities specified by the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare as actually taking those measures.

rules.parameter: Nutrition Labeling, Health Claims, Special Dietary Uses
rules.limit: Verification required
rules.notes: Specific regulations apply to nutrition labeling, food with health claims, and food for special dietary uses.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food with Health Claims, Food for Special Dietary Uses, and Nutrition Labeling

rules.limit: N/A
rules.notes: Food business operators must endeavor to record and retain necessary information, such as the name of the person who sold food/raw materials, to prevent food sanitation hazards.
rules.evidenceQuote: A food business operator must endeavor to make a record of any necessary information such as the name of the person who has sold food for sale, etc. or raw materials thereof to the food business operator and retain the record...

rules.parameter: Specifications and Standards for Food, Food Additives, etc.
rules.limit: Verification required
rules.sourceTitle: Specifications and Standards for Food, Food Additives, etc. Under The Food Sanitation Act
rules.notes: Compliance with the 'Specifications and Standards for Food, Food Additives, etc. Under The Food Sanitation Act' is required. These standards cover various aspects including ingredients, production methods, and limits for food and additives.
rules.evidenceQuote: Specifications and Standards for Food, Food Additives, etc. Under The Food Sanitation Act

rules.limit: Prohibited for sale
rules.notes: Prohibited for sale, collection, production, import, processing, use, cooking, storage, or display for sale, if contaminated with pathogens or suspected to be so, and likely to cause harm.
rules.evidenceQuote: The following food and additives must not be sold... (iii) those which are contaminated with pathogens or are suspected to be so, and which are likely to cause harm to human health;

rules.limit: Prohibited for sale
rules.notes: Prohibited for sale, collection, production, import, processing, use, cooking, storage, or display for sale, unless unlikely to cause harm and recognized as edible.
rules.evidenceQuote: The following food and additives must not be sold... (i) those which are rotten or have deteriorated, or are immature; provided, however, that this does not apply to those which are unlikely to cause harm to human health in general and are recognized as edible;

rules.notes: Suggested if there is a risk of cross-contamination from allergenic ingredients/additives.
rules.evidenceQuote: the following statement of precautionary allergen labeling is suggested: “ The plant, equipment or production line used to produce the product is also used to process○○” or the synonymous terms.

rules.parameter: γ-Aminobutyric Acid (GABA) produced by microbial fermentation
rules.limit: Verification required against the 'Use Restrictions and Labeling Requirements of γ-Aminobutyric Acid Produced by Microbial Fermentation as a Food Ingredient'
rules.sourceTitle: Use Restrictions and Labeling Requirements of γ-Aminobutyric Acid Produced by Microbial Fermentation as a Food Ingredient
rules.notes: Applies to GABA as a food ingredient.
rules.evidenceQuote: Use Restrictions and Labeling Requirements of γ-Aminobutyric Acid Produced by Microbial Fermentation as a Food Ingredient

rules.limit: Verification required for permitted uses and limits
rules.evidenceQuote: วัตถุเจือปนอาหาร

rules.limit: Verification required for specific allergen declaration requirements as part of food labeling
rules.notes: Inferred from general food labeling requirements, as allergen declaration is a critical component.
rules.evidenceQuote: การแสดงฉลากอาหารและฉลากโภชนาการ

rules.limit: Must comply with food standards for contaminants
rules.evidenceQuote: มาตรฐานอาหารที่มีสารปนเปื้อน

rules.parameter: Toxic Substances and Veterinary Drug Residues
rules.limit: Must comply with food standards for toxic substances and veterinary drug residues; verification required for specific MRLs
rules.evidenceQuote: มาตรฐานอาหารที่มีสารพิษ-ยาสัตว์ตกค้าง

rules.limit: Must comply with food labeling and nutrition labeling requirements
rules.evidenceQuote: การแสดงฉลากอาหารและฉลากโภชนาการ

rules.limit: Must comply with standards for pathogenic microorganisms
rules.evidenceQuote: มาตรฐานอาหารด้านจุลินทรีย์ที่ทำให้เกิดโรค

rules.limit: Verification required against FDA regulations and inventories
rules.notes: Manufacturers must verify the regulatory status and approved uses of food and color additives by consulting FDA listings and CFR citations.
rules.evidenceQuote: Approved Food and Color Additives and Petitions Under Review; Code of Federal Regulations (CFR) Citations

rules.limit: Delisted (not permitted for use)
rules.sourceTitle: Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS) | FDA
rules.notes: Refer to 21 CFR 81.10 and/or 81.30 for details.
rules.evidenceQuote: Delisted color additives in FDA regulations (21 CFR 81.10 and/or 81.30) (labeled as 'DELISTED')

rules.parameter: Food additives, color additives, GRAS substances, prior-sanctioned substances, flavoring substances
rules.limit: Verification required against FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 172, 173, 73, 74, 182, 184, 181) and inventories
rules.sourceTitle: Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS) | FDA
rules.notes: The 'Substances Added to Food inventory' includes these types of ingredients and serves as a reference.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives and color additives that are listed in FDA regulations... Generally Recognized as Safe ('GRAS') substances... prior-sanctioned substances

rules.limit: Prohibited from use in food (with exceptions as noted in regulations)
rules.sourceTitle: Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS) | FDA
rules.notes: Refer to 21 CFR Part 189 for details on prohibited substances.
rules.evidenceQuote: Prohibited substances that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189) as prohibited from use in food

rules.limit: Milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame
rules.notes: Sesame was added as the 9th major food allergen effective January 1, 2023, by the FASTER Act.
rules.evidenceQuote: The major food allergens are milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame.

rules.notes: This is a Guidance Level.
rules.evidenceQuote: 3-Monochloropropane-1,2-Diol (3-MCPD) Acid-hydrolyzed protein and Asian-style sauces Guidance Level 1 ppm

rules.notes: This is an Action Level. Specific action levels also exist for Brazil nuts, peanuts and peanut products, and pistachio nuts.
rules.evidenceQuote: Aflatoxins, total Foods Action Level 20 ppb

rules.parameter: Poisonous or deleterious substances (e.g., toxic elements, mycotoxins, persistent pesticides, radionuclides)
rules.limit: Verification required against the Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool, guidance documents, and regulations (21 CFR parts 109 and 509)
rules.notes: The tool provides a consolidated list of tolerances, action levels, guidance levels, derived intervention levels, recommended maximum levels, and advisory levels.
rules.evidenceQuote: The Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool provides a consolidated list of these levels. Tolerances and action levels are defined in 21 Code of Federal Regulations (CFR) parts 109 and 509.

rules.limit: Must meet defined standard for gluten content. Compliance requirements for fermented and hydrolyzed foods.
rules.notes: The FDA issued a final rule defining 'gluten-free' for food labeling and later established compliance requirements for fermented and hydrolyzed foods.
rules.evidenceQuote: On August 2, 2013, the FDA issued a final rule defining 'gluten-free' for food labeling... On August 12, 2020, the FDA issued a final rule to establish compliance requirements for fermented and hydrolyzed foods

rules.limit: Not required by law; should not be used as a substitute for adhering to current good manufacturing practices; must be truthful and not misleading
rules.notes: Such statements are used to address unavoidable 'cross-contact' only if manufacturers have incorporated good manufacturing processes and taken every precaution.
rules.evidenceQuote: Such statements are not required by law and can be used to address unavoidable 'cross-contact,' only if manufacturers have incorporated good manufacturing processes... must be truthful and not misleading.

rules.foodCategory: Human food (establishments that manufacture, process, pack, or hold)
rules.limit: Establishments must put written procedures in place to control allergen cross-contact and ensure accurate allergen labeling
rules.notes: Part of the 'Current Good Manufacturing Practice, Hazard Analysis, and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food' rule (CGMP & PC rule, 21 CFR part 117).
rules.evidenceQuote: The CGMP & PC rule includes requirements for allergen preventive controls to prevent allergen cross-contact in manufacturing and packaging and to prevent undeclared allergens.

rules.limit: Must be declared on the label
rules.notes: Identified as allergenic substances.
rules.evidenceQuote: Color additives made from cochineal extract and carmine... must be declared on the label of all food and cosmetic products.

rules.limit: Must be declared in parentheses after ingredient name OR in a 'contains' statement immediately after/next to ingredient list. Specific types of tree nuts, fish, and Crustacean shellfish must be declared.
rules.notes: This requirement is mandated by FALCPA and the FASTER Act.
rules.evidenceQuote: The allergen's food source must be declared at least once on the food label in one of two ways... type of tree nut... species of fish... and Crustacean shellfish... to be declared.

rules.foodCategory: All products containing FD&C Yellow No. 5
rules.limit: Must be identified on labels
rules.notes: Required due to potential hypersensitivity reactions in some individuals.
rules.evidenceQuote: The FDA requires all products containing FD&C Yellow No. 5 to identify it on their labels

rules.limit: Must be declared on food labels when concentration is ≥10 parts per million total sulfites
rules.notes: Required due to adverse reactions in sensitive individuals (e.g., asthma).
rules.evidenceQuote: they must be declared on food labels when present in food and the concentration in the food is ≥10 parts per million total sulfites.
